common_tongues: Common speech cannot be assumed globally uniform; usage should follow region and power structure.
prestige_languages: Prestige, court, clerical, and administrative speech may differ from ordinary local speech.
ritual_languages: Religious and ceremonial contexts may preserve distinct formulaic language.
literacy_expectations: Literacy must be inferred conservatively from institution, class, and polity context.
dialect_pressure: Local diction, register, and naming pressure should vary by region and settlement type.
naming_conventions: Names should preserve canon forms and local patterning where supported.
stranger_address_norms: Forms of address should reflect class, law, faith, and local custom rather than generic fantasy assumptions.
justification: Repository synthesis layer built to make strongly supported canon operational without overriding source truth.
